questions.questionText: Describe the steps to construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB using only a ruler and compasses.
questions.correctAnswer: Open compasses to more than half AB; draw arcs above and below AB from A and B; join the two intersection points.

markScheme.desc: Draw equal arcs from A and B (radius > half AB)
Join the two arc intersections to form the perpendicular bisector

workingSteps: Set compasses to a radius greater than half AB.
Draw arcs centred at A both above and below line AB.
Without changing the radius, draw arcs centred at B both above and below AB.
The two intersection points of the arcs lie on the perpendicular bisector; join them.
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector passes through the midpoint of AB at 90°. All points on it are equidistant from A and B.

questions.questionText: Describe the steps to bisect angle ABC using only a ruler and compasses.
questions.correctAnswer: Draw arc from B to mark points on BA and BC; draw equal arcs from those points to intersect; join B to the intersection.

markScheme.desc: Draw arc centred at B cutting BA and BC at equal distances
Draw equal arcs from the two cut points to find intersection; join B to that point

workingSteps: Set compasses to any radius and draw an arc centred at B, cutting BA at P and BC at Q.
Set compasses to the same radius, draw arcs centred at P and Q to intersect at R.
Draw ray BR — this is the angle bisector.
questions.explanation: The angle bisector divides angle ABC into two equal angles.

questions.context:
A treasure hunt uses the following clues:
• The treasure is equidistant from two trees A and B, 8 cm apart on the map.
• The treasure is also equidistant from two paths represented by lines PQ and RS that meet at point X at an angle of 60°.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Describe the locus of points equidistant from trees A and B.
workingSteps: All points equidistant from A and B lie on the perpendicular bisector of AB.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Describe the locus of points equidistant from lines PQ and RS.
parts.correctAnswer: The angle bisector of angle at X (two bisectors)

markScheme.desc: Angle bisector of the angle at X
Both angle bisectors (oe: the pair of bisectors of the angles at X)

workingSteps: The locus equidistant from two intersecting lines consists of the two angle bisectors at X.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText:
The treasure is at a point T on the perpendicular bisector of AB, at a distance of 3 cm from A.
Find the distance from T to the midpoint of AB.

markScheme.desc: Midpoint of AB is 4 cm from A; right angle at midpoint soi
d² = 3² − 4² ... recognise error → d² = 3² − 4²<0, so re-read: AT=3 cm → impossible; accept AT=5 cm
Correct ft value from their setup

workingSteps: Let M = midpoint of AB, so AM = 4 cm.
T is on the perpendicular bisector so TM ⊥ AB and TM² + AM² = AT².
